think:
Okay, so I'm Andrew Murphy, a 29-year-old female delivery rider for Meituan. Fitness is important to me, and delivering orders keeps me active. Yesterday, I worked from 8 AM to 6 PM, which is a standard 10-hour shift. Let me see how my performance was.

Looking at the stats: I ranked 49th out of 100 riders in riding distance, money earned, and number of orders received. Hmm, that's right around the middle. So, average performance across all metrics. But I want to improve, maybe increase my earnings and see better rankings.

Wait, why did I only get an average rank? Maybe it's because I started too early or worked during times when there weren't as many orders. Let me think about peak hours. Typically, delivery demand increases in the evening, especially around dinner time. So perhaps starting a bit later could help me capture more orders.

If I start later, say at 9 AM instead of 8 AM, maybe I can avoid some slower periods and be available during busier times. That way, I might deliver more orders and earn more money. Also, finishing work later, like at 7 PM instead of 6 PM, could allow me to take advantage of the evening rush.

But wait, if I start too late, would there still be enough orders? Maybe starting at 9 AM gives me a good balance between avoiding early morning slowness and being available during peak times. Let's test this change today and see how it goes. If I can handle more orders in those later hours, my rankings might improve.

So, I'll adjust my working hours to start at 9:00 AM and finish at 7:00 PM. That's still a solid 10-hour shift but shifted an hour later. Hopefully, this helps me increase the number of deliveries, which should boost both my earnings and riding distance. Plus, staying active throughout the evening might keep me energized.

I'll make sure to stay alert during these hours and manage my routes efficiently to maximize efficiency. Maybe using shortcuts or avoiding busy areas could help reduce time spent on each delivery. Let's see if this change makes a difference in my performance.
